topic: Someone discovers a hidden room in their new house
raw_output:
[story 1]

The realtor had promised a charming, quiet suburban home. Instead, Sarah found herself navigating a suffocating stillness. Every creak of the floorboards above, every shadow stretching too long in the late afternoon sun, seemed amplified in the cavernous living room. Her hand, gripping the edge of the antique mahogany cabinet, was slick with a cold sweat despite the air conditioning humming dutifully. She’d been meticulously unpacking boxes, trying to impose order on the overwhelming emptiness, but a knot tightened low in her stomach with every passing minute. Then, tracing a faint outline behind a loose panel near the fireplace, her fingers brushed against something yielding. With a sharp intake of breath, she pushed. A section of the wall swung inward with a soft, grinding sound, revealing an opening swallowed by absolute darkness.

[story 2]

Mark kept running a hand over the back of his neck, his jaw clenched so tightly he could feel the muscles vibrating beneath his skin. The house felt wrong—too large, too silent, like a meticulously staged set waiting for a performance that hadn't started. He’d spent the entire day feeling an insistent prickling sensation on his skin, a low-level hum of unease that refused to dissipate. He was supposed to be admiring the view from the upstairs landing, but his gaze kept drifting to the study, a room he hadn't dared enter. As he leaned against the doorframe, his eyes caught a seam in the wallpaper near the baseboard. Hesitantly, he pressed. The panel gave way with a reluctant sigh, revealing a narrow passage descending into shadow, smelling faintly of dust and something metallic.

[story 3]

"Just settling in," Liam murmured to himself, his voice sounding thin and reedy in the echoing silence of the foyer. He kept glancing toward the hallway, his posture rigid, his shoulders hiked up around his ears. The sheer scale of the property was unnerving; the shadows pooled in the corners like liquid ink. He was trying to focus on assembling the bookshelf, but his concentration kept fracturing, pulling his attention toward the wall adjacent to the dining room. A subtle shift in the texture caught his eye—a slight misalignment in the molding. He moved closer, his heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against his ribs. When he applied a little pressure, a section of the paneling slid inward, revealing a cramped, unlit space beyond.
